-- ssa_to_stencil.lua -- lower optimized semantic SSA into hole-parametric Stencil IR.

local SIR = require("src.stencil_ir")
local IR = require("src.ssa_ir")
local Facts = require("src.facts")

local M = {}

local FIELD_KIND = { a = 1, b = 2, c = 3, dest = 4, aux = 5, cur = 0 }

local SLOT_FIELDS_BY_OP = {
  MOVE={"a","b"},
  LOADI={"a"}, LOADF={"a"}, LOADK={"a"}, LOADKX={"a"}, LOADFALSE={"a"}, LFALSESKIP={"a"}, LOADTRUE={"a"}, LOADNIL={"a"},
  GETUPVAL={"a"}, SETUPVAL={"a"},
  GETTABLE={"a","b","c"}, GETI={"a","b"}, GETFIELD={"a","b"}, GETTABUP={"a"}, SELF={"a","b"},
  SETTABLE={"a","b","c"}, SETI={"a","c"}, SETFIELD={"a","c"}, SETTABUP={"b","c"},
  NEWTABLE={"a"},
  ADD={"a","b","c"}, SUB={"a","b","c"}, MUL={"a","b","c"}, MOD={"a","b","c"}, POW={"a","b","c"}, DIV={"a","b","c"}, IDIV={"a","b","c"},
  BAND={"a","b","c"}, BOR={"a","b","c"}, BXOR={"a","b","c"}, SHL={"a","b","c"}, SHR={"a","b","c"},
  ADDI={"a","b"}, SHLI={"a","b"}, SHRI={"a","b"},
  ADDK={"a","b"}, SUBK={"a","b"}, MULK={"a","b"}, MODK={"a","b"}, POWK={"a","b"}, DIVK={"a","b"}, IDIVK={"a","b"},
  BANDK={"a","b"}, BORK={"a","b"}, BXORK={"a","b"},
  UNM={"a","b"}, BNOT={"a","b"}, NOT={"a","b"}, LEN={"a","b"}, CONCAT={"a","b","c"},
  EQ={"a","b","dest"}, LT={"a","b","dest"}, LE={"a","b","dest"}, EQK={"a"}, EQI={"a","dest"}, LTI={"a","dest"}, LEI={"a","dest"}, GTI={"a","dest"}, GEI={"a","dest"},
  TEST={"a","b"}, TESTSET={"a","b"},
  CALL={"a"}, TAILCALL={"a"}, RETURN={"a"}, RETURN1={"a"},
  SETLIST={"a"}, CLOSURE={"a"}, VARARG={"a"}, GETVARG={"a"},
  MMBIN={"a","b"}, MMBINI={"a"}, MMBINK={"a","b"}, CLOSE={"a"}, TBC={"a"}, ERRNNIL={"a"},
}
M.FIELD_KIND = FIELD_KIND

local function copy_array(xs)
  local out = {}
  for i, x in ipairs(xs or {}) do out[i] = x end
  return out
end

local function slot_number(slot)
  if type(slot) == "number" then return slot end
  if type(slot) == "string" then return tonumber(slot:match("^R(%d+)$") or slot:match("^(%d+)$")) end
  return nil
end

local function source_op_name(op)
  return type(op) == "table" and tostring(op.op or "") or tostring(op or "")
end

local function build_slot_classes(source_ops)
  local concrete_to_class, class_to_concrete, occurrences = {}, {}, {}
  local function class_for(conc)
    conc = tonumber(conc)
    if not conc then return nil end
    if concrete_to_class[conc] == nil then
      local cls = #class_to_concrete
      concrete_to_class[conc] = cls
      class_to_concrete[#class_to_concrete + 1] = conc
    end
    return concrete_to_class[conc]
  end
  for pc, op in ipairs(source_ops or {}) do
    if type(op) == "table" then
      for _, field in ipairs(SLOT_FIELDS_BY_OP[source_op_name(op)] or {}) do
        local conc = tonumber(op[field])
        if conc then
          local cls = class_for(conc)
          occurrences[cls] = occurrences[cls] or {}
          occurrences[cls][#occurrences[cls] + 1] = {op_idx = pc - 1, field_kind = FIELD_KIND[field] or 0, concrete = conc}
        end
      end
    end
  end
  return concrete_to_class, class_to_concrete, occurrences
end

local function const_role_for_source(source_ops, pc, args)
  local src = source_ops and source_ops[pc]
  local on = source_op_name(src)
  if on == "LOADI" or on == "LOADF" then return "imm", "imm" end
  if on == "ADDI" or on == "SHLI" or on == "SHRI" then return "imm", "sC" end
  if on:match("K$") or on == "EQK" then return "const", "k_i64" end
  if on == "JMP" then return "imm", "sJ" end
  return nil, nil
end

function M.lower(ssa_result_or_graph, source_ops, config)
  config = config or {}
  local g = ssa_result_or_graph.graph or ssa_result_or_graph
  source_ops = source_ops or ssa_result_or_graph.source_ops or {}
  local st = SIR.new(source_ops, config)
  local concrete_to_class, class_to_concrete, occurrences = build_slot_classes(source_ops)
  st.slot_class_by_concrete = concrete_to_class
  st.slot_concrete_by_class = class_to_concrete

  for cls, occs in pairs(occurrences) do
    for _, occ in ipairs(occs) do st:add_slotmap(occ.op_idx, cls, occ.field_kind) end
  end

  local function slot_class(slot, pc)
    local conc = slot_number(slot)
    local cls
    if conc then cls = concrete_to_class[conc] end
    if cls == nil then
      cls = #class_to_concrete
      class_to_concrete[#class_to_concrete + 1] = conc or cls
      if conc then concrete_to_class[conc] = cls end
    end
    if not occurrences[cls] then
      occurrences[cls] = {{op_idx = math.max(0, (pc or 1) - 1), field_kind = FIELD_KIND.cur, concrete = conc or cls}}
      st:add_slotmap(occurrences[cls][1].op_idx, cls, FIELD_KIND.cur)
    end
    return cls
  end

  local function canonical_guard(guard)
    if not guard or not guard.fact then return guard end
    local f = guard.fact
    local conc = slot_number(f.subject and f.subject.id)
    if conc == nil then return guard end
    local cls = slot_class(conc, 1)
    local nf = {}
    for k, v in pairs(f) do nf[k] = v end
    nf.subject = Facts.slot("R" .. tostring(cls))
    local ng = {}
    for k, v in pairs(guard) do ng[k] = v end
    ng.fact = nf
    ng.key = Facts.guard_key(nf)
    return ng
  end

  local function hole_for_slot(kind, slot, pc)
    local cls = slot_class(slot, pc)
    local occ = (occurrences[cls] and occurrences[cls][1]) or {op_idx = math.max(0, (pc or 1) - 1)}
    -- Prefer same-op occurrence when possible; runtime slot patching resolves by op_idx.
    for _, o in ipairs(occurrences[cls] or {}) do if o.op_idx == math.max(0, (pc or 1) - 1) then occ = o; break end end
    return st:hole({ role_kind = kind, role_arg = cls, op_idx = occ.op_idx, ty = "slot", key = kind .. ":" .. tostring(cls) .. ":" .. tostring(occ.op_idx) })
  end

  local vmap = {}
  local function map_value(vid)
    if not vid then return nil end
    if not vmap[vid] then
      local vv = g.values and g.values[vid]
      vmap[vid] = st:new_value(vv and vv.ty or "Unknown", vid, vv and vv.residency, vv and vv.facts)
    end
    return vmap[vid]
  end
  local function new_output(vid)
    local vv = g.values and g.values[vid]
    local out = st:new_value(vv and vv.ty or "Unknown", vid, vv and vv.residency, vv and vv.facts)
    vmap[vid] = out
    return out
  end

  local consumers = {}
  for _, n in ipairs(g.nodes or {}) do if not n.removed then
    for _, v in ipairs(n.inputs or {}) do consumers[v] = (consumers[v] or 0) + 1 end
  end end

  local skip = {}
  for idx, n in ipairs(g.nodes or {}) do
    if n.removed or skip[n.id] then goto continue end
    local pc = n.source or 1
    local op, args = n.op, n.args or {}
    local ins, outs = {}, {}
    for _, v in ipairs(n.inputs or {}) do ins[#ins + 1] = map_value(v) end
    for _, v in ipairs(n.outputs or {}) do outs[#outs + 1] = new_output(v) end

    if op == "FrameLoad" then
      local h = hole_for_slot("slot", args.slot or "cur", pc)
      st:add("LoadSlot", { outputs = outs, source = pc, hole = h, args = { slot = h.role_arg }, effect = "frame_read" })

    elseif op == "FrameStore" then
      local h = hole_for_slot("slot_store", args.slot or "cur", pc)
      st:add("StoreSlot", { inputs = ins, source = pc, hole = h, args = { slot = h.role_arg }, effect = "frame_write" })

    elseif op == "BoxI64" then
      local fused = nil
      for j = idx + 1, #g.nodes do
        local m = g.nodes[j]
        if not m.removed then
          if m.op == "FrameStore" and m.inputs and m.inputs[1] == (n.outputs and n.outputs[1]) then fused = m end
          break
        end
      end
      if fused then
        local h = hole_for_slot("slot_store", fused.args and fused.args.slot or "cur", fused.source or pc)
        local box_out = n.outputs and n.outputs[1]
        local keep_tvalue = (consumers[box_out] or 0) > 1
        local fused_outs = keep_tvalue and outs or {}
        st:add("StoreI64Slot", { inputs = ins, outputs = fused_outs, source = fused.source or pc, hole = h, args = { slot = h.role_arg }, effect = "frame_write" })
        skip[fused.id] = true
      else
        st:add("BoxI64Scratch", { inputs = ins, outputs = outs, source = pc })
      end

    elseif op == "GuardTypeI64" then
      st:add("GuardI64", { inputs = ins, source = pc, guard = canonical_guard(n.guard), deps = n.deps, exit = n.exit, effect = "guard" })
    elseif op == "GuardTable" then
      st:add("GuardTable", { inputs = ins, source = pc, guard = canonical_guard(n.guard), deps = n.deps, exit = n.exit, effect = "guard" })
    elseif op == "GuardShape" then
      local off = st:hole({ role_kind = "shape_offset", role = "shape_offset", op_idx = 0, ty = "offset", patchable = false, semantic = true })
      local sid = st:hole({ role_kind = "shape_id", role = "shape_id", op_idx = 0, ty = "u32", patchable = false, semantic = true })
      st:add("GuardShape", { inputs = ins, source = pc, guard = canonical_guard(n.guard), deps = n.deps, exit = n.exit, effect = "guard", args = { shape_offset = off.id, shape_id = sid.id } })
    elseif op == "GuardMetatableAbsent" then
      local off = st:hole({ role_kind = "metatable_offset", role = "metatable_offset", op_idx = 0, ty = "offset", patchable = false, semantic = true })
      st:add("GuardMetatableAbsent", { inputs = ins, source = pc, guard = canonical_guard(n.guard), deps = n.deps, exit = n.exit, effect = "guard", args = { metatable_offset = off.id } })
    elseif op == "GuardArrayHit" then
      st:add("GuardArrayHit", { inputs = ins, source = pc, guard = canonical_guard(n.guard), deps = n.deps, exit = n.exit, effect = "guard" })
    elseif op == "GuardBounds" then
      st:add("GuardBounds", { inputs = ins, source = pc, guard = canonical_guard(n.guard), deps = n.deps, exit = n.exit, effect = "guard" })
    elseif op == "GuardCallTarget" then
      local target = st:hole({ role_kind = "call_target", role = "call_target", op_idx = 0, ty = "ptr", patchable = false, semantic = true })
      st:add("GuardCallTarget", { inputs = ins, source = pc, guard = canonical_guard(n.guard), deps = n.deps, exit = n.exit, effect = "guard", args = { call_target = target.id } })

    elseif op == "ConstI64" then
      local rk, role = const_role_for_source(source_ops, pc, args)
      if rk then
        local h = st:hole({ role_kind = rk, role = role, op_idx = math.max(0, pc - 1), ty = "i64", semantic = false, key = role .. ":" .. tostring(math.max(0, pc - 1)) })
        st:add("ConstI64Hole", { outputs = outs, source = pc, hole = h, args = { role = role } })
      else
        st:add("ConstI64", { outputs = outs, source = pc, args = { value = tonumber(args.value) or 0 } })
      end
    elseif op == "ConstF64" then
      local rk, role = const_role_for_source(source_ops, pc, args)
      if rk then
        local h = st:hole({ role_kind = rk, role = role, op_idx = math.max(0, pc - 1), ty = "f64", semantic = false, key = role .. ":f64:" .. tostring(math.max(0, pc - 1)) })
        st:add("ConstF64Hole", { outputs = outs, source = pc, hole = h, args = { role = role } })
      else
        st:add("ConstF64", { outputs = outs, source = pc, args = { value = tonumber(args.value) or 0 } })
      end
    elseif op == "LoadConst" then
      local h = st:hole({ role_kind = "const", role = "k_idx", op_idx = math.max(0, pc - 1), ty = "const", semantic = false, key = "k_idx:" .. tostring(math.max(0, pc - 1)) })
      st:add("LoadConst", { outputs = outs, source = pc, hole = h, args = { const = args.const } })
    elseif op == "ConstNil" then st:add("ConstNil", { outputs = outs, source = pc })
    elseif op == "ConstBool" then
      local h = st:hole({ role_kind = "bool", role = "bool_val", op_idx = math.max(0, pc - 1), ty = "bool", semantic = false, key = "bool:" .. tostring(pc) })
      st:add("ConstBool", { outputs = outs, source = pc, hole = h, args = { value = args.value and true or false } })
    elseif op == "Move" then st:add("Move", { inputs = ins, outputs = outs, source = pc })
    elseif op == "NotTValue" then st:add("NotTValue", { inputs = ins, outputs = outs, source = pc })
    elseif op == "UnboxI64" then st:add("UnboxI64", { inputs = ins, outputs = outs, source = pc })
    elseif op == "AddI64" or op == "SubI64" or op == "MulI64" or op == "I64BinOp" or op == "I64UnaryOp" or op == "CmpI64" then
      st:add(op, { inputs = ins, outputs = outs, source = pc, args = args })
    elseif op == "FieldLoad" then
      local h = st:hole({ role_kind = "field_offset", role = "field_offset", op_idx = math.max(0, pc - 1), ty = "offset", patchable = false, semantic = true })
      st:add("FieldLoad", { inputs = ins, outputs = outs, source = pc, hole = h, args = { key = args.key }, effect = "heap_read" })
    elseif op == "FieldStore" then
      local h = st:hole({ role_kind = "field_offset", role = "field_offset", op_idx = math.max(0, pc - 1), ty = "offset", patchable = false, semantic = true })
      st:add("FieldStore", { inputs = ins, source = pc, hole = h, args = { key = args.key }, effect = "heap_write" })
    elseif op == "ArrayLoad" then
      local h = st:hole({ role_kind = "array_base_offset", role = "array_base_offset", op_idx = math.max(0, pc - 1), ty = "offset", patchable = false, semantic = true })
      st:add("ArrayLoad", { inputs = ins, outputs = outs, source = pc, hole = h, effect = "heap_read" })
    elseif op == "ArrayStore" then
      local h = st:hole({ role_kind = "array_base_offset", role = "array_base_offset", op_idx = math.max(0, pc - 1), ty = "offset", patchable = false, semantic = true })
      st:add("ArrayStore", { inputs = ins, source = pc, hole = h, effect = "heap_write" })
    elseif op == "BarrierCheck" then
      local h = st:hole({ role_kind = "barrier", role = "barrier", op_idx = math.max(0, pc - 1), ty = "bool", patchable = false, semantic = true })
      st:add("BarrierCheck", { inputs = ins, source = pc, hole = h, exit = n.exit, effect = "gc_barrier" })
    elseif op == "Boundary" or op == "Jump" or op == "Return1" or op == "Return0" or op == "Call" or op == "KnownCall" or op == "TailCall" then
      st:add("ExitBoundary", { inputs = ins, source = pc, args = { op = op, opcode = args.opcode, reason = args.reason }, exit = n.exit, effect = n.effect or "branch" })
    else
      error("unsupported SSA op reached stencil lowering: " .. tostring(op))
    end
    ::continue::
  end

  table.sort(st.slotmaps, function(a, b)
    if a.op_idx ~= b.op_idx then return a.op_idx < b.op_idx end
    if a.logical_slot ~= b.logical_slot then return a.logical_slot < b.logical_slot end
    return a.field_kind < b.field_kind
  end)
  return st
end

return M

-- stencil_lower.lua -- lower semantic Stencil IR to native stencil descriptors.

local StencilDesc = require("src.stencil_desc")
local Abi = require("src.stencil_abi_x64")
local Projection = require("src.stencil_projection")
local Contract = require("src.ssa_contract")
local NativeX64 = require("src.stencil_native_x64")

local M = {}

local SUPPORTED_OP = {
  LoadSlot = true,
  StoreSlot = true,
  StoreI64Slot = true,
  LoadConst = true,
  ConstNil = true,
  ConstBool = true,
  Move = true,
  NotTValue = true,
  GuardI64 = true,
  UnboxI64 = true,
  ConstI64 = true,
  ConstI64Hole = true,
  ConstF64 = true,
  ConstF64Hole = true,
  AddI64 = true,
  SubI64 = true,
  MulI64 = true,
  I64BinOp = true,
  I64UnaryOp = true,
  ExitBoundary = true,
}
M.SUPPORTED_OP = SUPPORTED_OP

local SUPPORTED_DATA_ROLE = { slot = true, slot_store = true, imm = true, const = true, bool = true }
M.SUPPORTED_DATA_ROLE = SUPPORTED_DATA_ROLE

local function copy_array(xs)
  local out = {}
  for i, x in ipairs(xs or {}) do out[i] = x end
  return out
end

local function copy_contract(c)
  return {
    selector_sig = c.selector_sig,
    required_sig = c.required_sig,
    checked_sig = c.checked_sig,
    produced_sig = c.produced_sig,
    killed_sig = c.killed_sig,
    deps = copy_array(c.deps),
    exits = copy_array(c.exits),
  }
end

local function standard_locations(kind)
  -- Endpoint contracts are explicit even for the first native byte surface:
  -- entry/success/exit all agree on ctx in rdi and a synced frame in ctx.stack.
  -- Later linked-image endpoints can refine this without changing the public
  -- ABI shape.
  return {
    StencilDesc.location({ kind = "reg", reg = "rdi", value_type = "Ptr", name = "ctx" }),
    StencilDesc.location({ kind = "ctx_field", index = 0, value_type = "Ptr", name = "ctx.stack.synced_frame" }),
    StencilDesc.location({ kind = "immediate", index = kind == "entry" and 1 or 0, value_type = "Bool", name = "abstract_endpoint_contract" }),
  }
end

local function endpoint(endpoints, projections, kind, n)
  local start = #projections + 1
  local p = nil
  if kind ~= "entry" and kind ~= "ok" then
    p = Projection.for_exit(nil, n)
    local ok, perr = Projection.validate_projection(p)
    if not ok then return nil, perr end
    projections[#projections + 1] = p
  end
  local ep = StencilDesc.endpoint({
    kind = kind,
    source = n and n.source,
    exit = n and n.exit,
    contract = "ctx_rdi_synced_frame_abstract_v1",
    locations = standard_locations(kind),
    projection_start = kind ~= "entry" and kind ~= "ok" and start or nil,
    n_projections = kind ~= "entry" and kind ~= "ok" and 1 or 0,
    projections = p and { p } or {},
  })
  endpoints[#endpoints + 1] = ep
  return #endpoints
end

local EXIT_KIND_BY_OP = {
  ExitBoundary = "boundary_exit",
}

function M.lower_result(ssa_result, config)
  config = config or {}
  local errors = {}
  local st = ssa_result and ssa_result.stencil
  if not st then return nil, { "missing stencil" } end

  for _, n in ipairs(st.ops or {}) do
    if not SUPPORTED_OP[n.op] then errors[#errors + 1] = "unsupported native stencil op: " .. tostring(n.op) end
  end

  local contract = Contract.from_result(ssa_result, config.facts or ssa_result.facts or {})
  if #(contract.deps or {}) > 0 then errors[#errors + 1] = "unsupported dependencies in native stencil: " .. table.concat(contract.deps, ",") end

  for _, h in ipairs(st.holes or {}) do
    if h.role_kind == "exit" or h.role_kind == "fail" then
      errors[#errors + 1] = "control endpoint encoded as data hole: " .. tostring(h.role_kind)
    elseif not SUPPORTED_DATA_ROLE[h.role_kind] then
      errors[#errors + 1] = "unsupported native stencil data hole role: " .. tostring(h.role_kind)
    end
  end

  if #errors > 0 then return nil, errors end

  local endpoints, projections = {}, {}
  local exit_endpoint_by_node = {}
  endpoint(endpoints, projections, "entry", nil)
  local ok_endpoint_index = endpoint(endpoints, projections, "ok", nil)

  for _, n in ipairs(st.ops or {}) do
    if n.op == "GuardI64" then
      exit_endpoint_by_node[n.id] = assert(endpoint(endpoints, projections, "guard_exit", n))
    elseif EXIT_KIND_BY_OP[n.op] then
      exit_endpoint_by_node[n.id] = assert(endpoint(endpoints, projections, EXIT_KIND_BY_OP[n.op], n))
    end
  end

  local native, native_errors = NativeX64.emit(st, { exit_endpoint_by_node = exit_endpoint_by_node, ok_endpoint_index = ok_endpoint_index, endpoints = endpoints })
  if not native then return nil, native_errors end

  local semantic_mode = "inline"
  for _, n in ipairs(st.ops or {}) do if n.op == "ExitBoundary" then semantic_mode = "boundary"; break end end

  local abi = Abi.desc()
  local f = StencilDesc.stencil({
    name = "stencil_" .. tostring(ssa_result.stencil_hash or "unknown"),
    physical_abi = Abi.ID,
    pattern_key = ssa_result.stencil_hash,
    len = #(ssa_result.source_ops or st.source_ops or {}),
    executable = true,
    exact = true,
    semantic_mode = semantic_mode,
    layout = native.layout,
    code_hex = native.code_hex,
    code_size = native.code_size,
    clobbers = copy_array(abi.clobbers),
    endpoints = endpoints,
    data_relocs = native.data_relocs,
    control_relocs = native.control_relocs,
    slotmaps = copy_array(st.slotmaps),
    projections = projections,
    dependencies = copy_array(contract.deps),
    fact_transfer = copy_contract(contract),
    active_node_specs = copy_array(ssa_result.active_node_specs),
    stencil_ops = copy_array(ssa_result.stencil_ops),
    source_ops = copy_array(ssa_result.source_ops or st.source_ops),
  })

  local ok, verrs = StencilDesc.validate_stencil(f)
  if not ok then return nil, verrs end
  f.abi = StencilDesc.lower_to_abi(f)
  return f
end

function M.generate(ssa_result, config)
  local stencil, errors = M.lower_result(ssa_result, config)
  return { ok = stencil ~= nil, stencil = stencil, errors = errors or {} }
end

return M

-- stencil_ir.lua -- semantic stencil shape IR for stencil lowering.
--
-- Semantic SSA remains the Lua semantics authority. This module defines
-- hole-parametric operation shape consumed by normalization, contracts,
-- stencil metadata validation, and native emission.

local M = {}

local KNOWN_OP = {
  LoadSlot = true, StoreSlot = true, StoreI64Slot = true,
  LoadConst = true, ConstI64 = true, ConstI64Hole = true, ConstF64 = true, ConstF64Hole = true, ConstNil = true, ConstBool = true,
  Move = true, NotTValue = true, GuardI64 = true, GuardTable = true, GuardShape = true,
  GuardMetatableAbsent = true, GuardCallTarget = true, GuardArrayHit = true, GuardBounds = true,
  UnboxI64 = true, BoxI64Scratch = true,
  AddI64 = true, SubI64 = true, MulI64 = true, I64BinOp = true, I64UnaryOp = true, CmpI64 = true,
  FieldLoad = true, FieldStore = true, ArrayLoad = true, ArrayStore = true, BarrierCheck = true,
  ExitBoundary = true,
}
M.KNOWN_OP = KNOWN_OP

local DATA_HOLE_ROLE = {
  unknown = true,
  slot = true,
  slot_store = true,
  imm = true,
  const = true,
  bool = true,
  shape_offset = true,
  shape_id = true,
  metatable_offset = true,
  field_offset = true,
  array_base_offset = true,
  call_target = true,
  barrier = true,
}
M.DATA_HOLE_ROLE = DATA_HOLE_ROLE

local function copy_array(xs)
  local out = {}
  for i, x in ipairs(xs or {}) do out[i] = x end
  return out
end

local function role_name(kind, arg)
  if kind == "slot" then return "R" .. tostring(arg or 0) end
  if kind == "slot_store" then return "slot_R" .. tostring(arg or 0) end
  return tostring(kind or "unknown")
end

local Stencil = {}
Stencil.__index = Stencil
M.Stencil = Stencil

function M.new(source_ops, config)
  return setmetatable({
    ops = {},
    values = {},
    value_order = {},
    holes = {},
    hole_by_key = {},
    slotmaps = {},
    exits = {},
    facts = {},
    source_ops = copy_array(source_ops or {}),
    config = config or {},
    next_value = 1,
    next_hole = 0,
  }, Stencil)
end

function Stencil:new_value(ty, source, residency, facts)
  local id = "sv" .. tostring(self.next_value)
  self.next_value = self.next_value + 1
  local v = { id = id, ty = ty or "Unknown", source = source, residency = residency, facts = copy_array(facts) }
  self.values[id] = v
  self.value_order[#self.value_order + 1] = id
  return id
end

function Stencil:add_slotmap(op_idx, logical_slot, field_kind)
  if logical_slot == nil or field_kind == nil then return end
  logical_slot = tonumber(logical_slot)
  if not logical_slot or logical_slot < 0 or logical_slot >= 256 then return end
  local k = tostring(op_idx or 0) .. ":" .. tostring(logical_slot) .. ":" .. tostring(field_kind)
  self._slotmap_seen = self._slotmap_seen or {}
  if self._slotmap_seen[k] then return end
  self._slotmap_seen[k] = true
  self.slotmaps[#self.slotmaps + 1] = {
    op_idx = tonumber(op_idx or 0) or 0,
    logical_slot = logical_slot,
    field_kind = tonumber(field_kind or 0) or 0,
  }
end

function Stencil:hole(t)
  t = t or {}
  assert(t.role_kind ~= "exit" and t.role_kind ~= "fail", "exit/fail are control endpoints, not data holes")
  local key = t.key or table.concat({t.role_kind or "unknown", tostring(t.role_arg or ""), tostring(t.op_idx or 0), t.ty or "Unknown", tostring(t.semantic and 1 or 0)}, ":")
  local existing = self.hole_by_key[key]
  if existing then return existing end
  local id = self.next_hole
  self.next_hole = self.next_hole + 1
  local h = {
    id = id,
    role_kind = t.role_kind or "unknown",
    role = t.role or role_name(t.role_kind, t.role_arg),
    role_arg = t.role_arg,
    op_idx = tonumber(t.op_idx or 0) or 0,
    ty = t.ty or "uintptr",
    patchable = t.patchable ~= false and (t.patchable or DATA_HOLE_ROLE[t.role_kind or "unknown"] or false),
    semantic = t.semantic and true or false,
    key = key,
  }
  self.holes[#self.holes + 1] = h
  self.hole_by_key[key] = h
  return h
end

function Stencil:add(op, t)
  assert(KNOWN_OP[op], "unknown Stencil IR op: " .. tostring(op))
  t = t or {}
  local n = {
    id = #self.ops + 1,
    op = op,
    inputs = copy_array(t.inputs),
    outputs = copy_array(t.outputs),
    args = t.args or {},
    effect = t.effect or "none",
    source = t.source,
    hole = t.hole,
    guard = t.guard,
    exit = t.exit,
    deps = copy_array(t.deps),
  }
  self.ops[#self.ops + 1] = n
  if n.exit then self.exits[#self.exits + 1] = { op = op, source = n.source or 0, exit = n.exit } end
  return n
end

local function defined_values(st)
  local def = {}
  for _, id in ipairs(st.value_order or {}) do def[id] = true end
  return def
end

function M.validate(st)
  local errors = {}
  if not st then return false, {"missing stencil"} end
  local def = defined_values(st)
  for i, h in ipairs(st.holes or {}) do
    if h.id ~= i - 1 then errors[#errors + 1] = "non-contiguous hole id at index " .. i end
    if h.role_kind == "exit" or h.role_kind == "fail" then
      errors[#errors + 1] = "control endpoint encoded as data hole: " .. tostring(h.role_kind)
    end
    if (h.role_kind == "slot" or h.role_kind == "slot_store") then
      if h.role_arg == nil or tonumber(h.role_arg) == nil or tonumber(h.role_arg) < 0 or tonumber(h.role_arg) >= 8 then
        errors[#errors + 1] = "slot hole outside 0..7 canonical fact ABI: " .. tostring(h.role_arg)
      end
    end
  end
  for _, op in ipairs(st.ops or {}) do
    if not KNOWN_OP[op.op] then errors[#errors + 1] = "unknown op " .. tostring(op.op) end
    for _, v in ipairs(op.inputs or {}) do if not def[v] then errors[#errors + 1] = "unknown input " .. tostring(v) .. " at op " .. tostring(op.id) end end
    for _, v in ipairs(op.outputs or {}) do if not def[v] then errors[#errors + 1] = "unknown output " .. tostring(v) .. " at op " .. tostring(op.id) end end
  end
  local has_slotmap = {}
  for _, sm in ipairs(st.slotmaps or {}) do
    if sm.logical_slot == nil then errors[#errors + 1] = "slotmap without logical slot" end
    has_slotmap[tonumber(sm.logical_slot)] = true
  end
  for _, h in ipairs(st.holes or {}) do
    if (h.role_kind == "slot" or h.role_kind == "slot_store") and not has_slotmap[tonumber(h.role_arg)] then
      errors[#errors + 1] = "slot hole without matching slotmap: " .. tostring(h.role_arg)
    end
  end
  return #errors == 0, errors
end

return M

-- stencil_native_x64.lua -- emit executable x86-64 bytes for first SpongeJIT native stencil surface.
--
-- This module is deliberately small and explicit: it emits fixed-width patch
-- sites for canonical slot, immediate, and control relocations. The foundry
-- normal form remains the semantic identity; every per-instance value is a
-- relocation, never baked into representative source operands.

local StencilDesc = require("src.stencil_desc")

local M = {}

local LUA_VNUMINT = 3
local LUA_VNUMFLT = 19

local CTX_STACK = 0
local CTX_K = 8
local CTX_EXIT_KIND = 16 + 256 * 16
local CTX_EXIT_PC = CTX_EXIT_KIND + 4
local CTX_EXIT_OP_IDX = CTX_EXIT_KIND + 8
local CTX_EXIT_HOLE = CTX_EXIT_KIND + 12

local SPON_EXIT_NONE = 0
local SPON_EXIT_GUARD = 1
local SPON_EXIT_BOUNDARY = 2
local SPON_EXIT_RUNTIME_ERROR = 3

local REG = {
  rax = 0, rcx = 1, rdx = 2, rbx = 3, rsp = 4, rbp = 5, rsi = 6, rdi = 7,
  r8 = 8, r9 = 9, r10 = 10, r11 = 11,
}

local REG_POOL = { "rax", "rcx", "rdx", "r8", "r9", "r10" }

local BINOP = {
  ADD = "add",
  SUB = "sub",
  MUL = "imul",
  BAND = "and",
  BOR = "or",
  BXOR = "xor",
}

local CMP_CC = {
  EQ = 0x94, EQI = 0x94,
  LT = 0x9c, LTI = 0x9c,
  LE = 0x9e, LEI = 0x9e,
  GT = 0x9f, GTI = 0x9f,
  GE = 0x9d, GEI = 0x9d,
}

local function f64_bytes_from_int(value)
  local n = tonumber(value) or 0
  if n == 0 then return {0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0} end
  local sign = 0
  if n < 0 then sign = 0x80000000; n = -n end
  local exp = math.floor(math.log(n) / math.log(2))
  local pow = 2 ^ exp
  local mant = math.floor((n / pow - 1) * 2 ^ 52 + 0.5)
  local lo = mant % 4294967296
  local hi = sign + (exp + 1023) * 1048576 + math.floor(mant / 4294967296)
  return {
    lo % 256, math.floor(lo / 256) % 256, math.floor(lo / 65536) % 256, math.floor(lo / 16777216) % 256,
    hi % 256, math.floor(hi / 256) % 256, math.floor(hi / 65536) % 256, math.floor(hi / 16777216) % 256,
  }
end

local function copy_array(xs)
  local out = {}
  for i, x in ipairs(xs or {}) do out[i] = x end
  return out
end

local function u8s_from_u32(x)
  x = tonumber(x) or 0
  if x < 0 then x = x + 4294967296 end
  return x % 256, math.floor(x / 256) % 256, math.floor(x / 65536) % 256, math.floor(x / 16777216) % 256
end

local function u8s_from_u64(x)
  local n = tonumber(x) or 0
  local sign = n < 0
  if sign then n = -n end
  local bytes = {}
  for i = 1, 8 do
    bytes[i] = n % 256
    n = math.floor(n / 256)
  end
  if sign then
    local carry = 1
    for i = 1, 8 do
      local v = 255 - bytes[i] + carry
      if v >= 256 then v = v - 256; carry = 1 else carry = 0 end
      bytes[i] = v
    end
  end
  return bytes
end

local Asm = {}
Asm.__index = Asm

local function new_asm()
  return setmetatable({ bytes = {}, labels = {}, fixups = {}, errors = {} }, Asm)
end

function Asm:pos() return #self.bytes end
function Asm:emit(...) for i = 1, select("#", ...) do self.bytes[#self.bytes + 1] = select(i, ...) end end
function Asm:mark(name) self.labels[name] = self:pos() end
function Asm:error(msg) self.errors[#self.errors + 1] = msg end

function Asm:patch_u32(off, value)
  local b1, b2, b3, b4 = u8s_from_u32(value)
  self.bytes[off + 1], self.bytes[off + 2], self.bytes[off + 3], self.bytes[off + 4] = b1, b2, b3, b4
end

function Asm:rex(w, r, x, b)
  local v = 0x40
  if w then v = v + 0x08 end
  if r and r >= 8 then v = v + 0x04 end
  if x and x >= 8 then v = v + 0x02 end
  if b and b >= 8 then v = v + 0x01 end
  if v ~= 0x40 then self:emit(v) end
end

function Asm:modrm(mod, reg, rm)
  self:emit(mod * 64 + (reg % 8) * 8 + (rm % 8))
end

function Asm:disp32(v)
  local b1, b2, b3, b4 = u8s_from_u32(v or 0)
  self:emit(b1, b2, b3, b4)
end

function Asm:imm32(v) self:disp32(v) end
function Asm:imm64(v)
  for _, b in ipairs(u8s_from_u64(v or 0)) do self:emit(b) end
end

function Asm:imm64_bytes(bytes)
  for i = 1, 8 do self:emit((bytes and bytes[i]) or 0) end
end

function Asm:mov_r64_m64(dst, base, disp)
  dst, base = REG[dst], REG[base]
  self:rex(true, dst, nil, base); self:emit(0x8b); self:modrm(2, dst, base); local off = self:pos(); self:disp32(disp or 0); return off
end

function Asm:mov_m64_r64(base, disp, src)
  base, src = REG[base], REG[src]
  self:rex(true, src, nil, base); self:emit(0x89); self:modrm(2, src, base); local off = self:pos(); self:disp32(disp or 0); return off
end

function Asm:mov_r64_r64(dst, src)
  dst, src = REG[dst], REG[src]
  if dst == src then return end
  self:rex(true, src, nil, dst); self:emit(0x89); self:modrm(3, src, dst)
end

function Asm:movabs_r64(dst, imm)
  dst = REG[dst]
  self:rex(true, nil, nil, dst); self:emit(0xb8 + (dst % 8)); local off = self:pos(); self:imm64(imm or 0); return off
end

function Asm:movabs_r64_bytes(dst, bytes)
  dst = REG[dst]
  self:rex(true, nil, nil, dst); self:emit(0xb8 + (dst % 8)); local off = self:pos(); self:imm64_bytes(bytes); return off
end

function Asm:cmp_m8_imm8(base, disp, imm)
  base = REG[base]
  self:rex(false, nil, nil, base); self:emit(0x80); self:modrm(2, 7, base); local off = self:pos(); self:disp32(disp or 0); local imm_off = self:pos(); self:emit(imm or 0); return off, imm_off
end

function Asm:cmp_r8_imm8(reg, imm)
  reg = REG[reg]
  self:rex(false, nil, nil, reg); self:emit(0x80); self:modrm(3, 7, reg); self:emit(imm or 0)
end

function Asm:cmp_r64_imm8(reg, imm)
  reg = REG[reg]
  self:rex(true, nil, nil, reg); self:emit(0x83); self:modrm(3, 7, reg); self:emit((imm or 0) % 256)
end

function Asm:mov_r8_m8(dst, base, disp)
  dst, base = REG[dst], REG[base]
  self:rex(false, dst, nil, base); self:emit(0x8a); self:modrm(2, dst, base); local off = self:pos(); self:disp32(disp or 0); return off
end

function Asm:mov_m8_r8(base, disp, src)
  base, src = REG[base], REG[src]
  self:rex(false, src, nil, base); self:emit(0x88); self:modrm(2, src, base); local off = self:pos(); self:disp32(disp or 0); return off
end

function Asm:mov_m8_imm8(base, disp, imm)
  base = REG[base]
  self:rex(false, nil, nil, base); self:emit(0xc6); self:modrm(2, 0, base); local off = self:pos(); self:disp32(disp or 0); local imm_off = self:pos(); self:emit(imm or 0); return off, imm_off
end

function Asm:mov_m64_imm32(base, disp, imm)
  base = REG[base]
  self:rex(true, nil, nil, base); self:emit(0xc7); self:modrm(2, 0, base); local off = self:pos(); self:disp32(disp or 0); self:imm32(imm or 0); return off
end

function Asm:mov_m32_imm32(base, disp, imm)
  base = REG[base]
  self:rex(false, nil, nil, base); self:emit(0xc7); self:modrm(2, 0, base); self:disp32(disp or 0); self:imm32(imm or 0)
end

function Asm:bin_r64_r64(op, dst, src)
  local opcode = ({ add = 0x01, sub = 0x29, ["and"] = 0x21, ["or"] = 0x09, ["xor"] = 0x31 })[op]
  dst, src = REG[dst], REG[src]
  if op == "imul" then
    self:rex(true, dst, nil, src); self:emit(0x0f, 0xaf); self:modrm(3, dst, src)
  elseif opcode then
    self:rex(true, src, nil, dst); self:emit(opcode); self:modrm(3, src, dst)
  else
    self:error("unsupported x64 binop: " .. tostring(op))
  end
end

function Asm:unary_r64(op, reg)
  local ext = ({ UNM = 3, BNOT = 2 })[op]
  reg = REG[reg]
  if not ext then self:error("unsupported x64 unary op: " .. tostring(op)); return end
  self:rex(true, nil, nil, reg); self:emit(0xf7); self:modrm(3, ext, reg)
end

function Asm:neg_r64(reg)
  reg = REG[reg]
  self:rex(true, nil, nil, reg); self:emit(0xf7); self:modrm(3, 3, reg)
end

function Asm:shift_r64_cl(op, reg)
  local ext = ({ shl = 4, shr = 5 })[op]
  reg = REG[reg]
  if not ext then self:error("unsupported x64 shift op: " .. tostring(op)); return end
  self:rex(true, nil, nil, reg); self:emit(0xd3); self:modrm(3, ext, reg)
end

function Asm:cmp_r64_r64(left, right)
  left, right = REG[left], REG[right]
  self:rex(true, right, nil, left); self:emit(0x39); self:modrm(3, right, left)
end

function Asm:test_r64_r64(reg)
  reg = REG[reg]
  self:rex(true, reg, nil, reg); self:emit(0x85); self:modrm(3, reg, reg)
end

function Asm:setcc_r8(cc, reg)
  reg = REG[reg]
  self:rex(false, nil, nil, reg); self:emit(0x0f, cc); self:modrm(3, 0, reg)
end

function Asm:movzx_r64_r8(dst, src)
  dst, src = REG[dst], REG[src]
  self:rex(true, dst, nil, src); self:emit(0x0f, 0xb6); self:modrm(3, dst, src)
end

function Asm:jcc32(cc, label)
  self:emit(0x0f, cc)
  local off = self:pos(); self:disp32(0)
  self.fixups[#self.fixups + 1] = { off = off, label = label }
  return off
end

function Asm:jmp32(label)
  self:emit(0xe9)
  local off = self:pos(); self:disp32(0)
  self.fixups[#self.fixups + 1] = { off = off, label = label }
  return off
end

function Asm:ret() self:emit(0xc3) end

function Asm:finish()
  for _, f in ipairs(self.fixups) do
    local target = self.labels[f.label]
    if not target then self:error("missing label: " .. tostring(f.label))
    else self:patch_u32(f.off, target - (f.off + 4)) end
  end
  if #self.errors > 0 then return nil, self.errors end
  local chars = {}
  for i, b in ipairs(self.bytes) do chars[i] = string.char(b) end
  return table.concat(chars)
end

local function bytes_to_hex(bytes)
  local out = {}
  for i = 1, #bytes do out[#out + 1] = string.format("%02x", bytes:byte(i)) end
  return table.concat(out)
end

local function use_counts(st)
  local counts = {}
  for _, n in ipairs(st.ops or {}) do
    for _, v in ipairs(n.inputs or {}) do counts[v] = (counts[v] or 0) + 1 end
  end
  return counts
end

local function slot_default_disp(h, addend)
  return (tonumber(h and h.role_arg) or 0) * 16 + (addend or 0)
end

local function make_data_reloc(out, code_offset, reloc_kind, h, addend)
  out[#out + 1] = StencilDesc.data_reloc({
    code_offset = code_offset,
    code_offset_kind = "x86_64_patch_site",
    reloc_kind = reloc_kind,
    role_kind = h and h.role_kind,
    op_idx = h and h.op_idx or 0,
    role_arg = h and h.role_arg or 0,
    hole_id = h and h.id,
    value_type = h and h.ty,
    addend = addend or 0,
  })
end

local function op_exit_kind(n)
  if n.op == "GuardI64" then return SPON_EXIT_GUARD end
  if n.op == "ExitBoundary" then return SPON_EXIT_BOUNDARY end
  return SPON_EXIT_RUNTIME_ERROR
end

local function control_edge_kind(n)
  if n.op == "GuardI64" then return "guard_fail" end
  return "boundary"
end

function M.emit(st, opts)
  opts = opts or {}
  local errors = {}
  local a = new_asm()
  local data_relocs, control_relocs = {}, {}
  local counts = use_counts(st)
  local loc, free = {}, {}
  for _, r in ipairs(REG_POOL) do free[r] = true end

  local function alloc(v)
    for _, r in ipairs(REG_POOL) do
      if free[r] then free[r] = nil; if v then loc[v] = { kind = "reg", reg = r } end; return r end
    end
    errors[#errors + 1] = "native x64 register budget exceeded"
    return nil
  end

  local function free_reg(r)
    if r and r ~= "r11" and REG[r] and not free[r] then free[r] = true end
  end

  local function consume(v)
    if not v then return end
    counts[v] = (counts[v] or 0) - 1
    if counts[v] <= 0 then
      local l = loc[v]
      if l and l.kind == "reg" then free_reg(l.reg) end
    end
  end

  local function input_reg(v)
    local l = loc[v]
    if not l then errors[#errors + 1] = "missing native value location: " .. tostring(v); return nil end
    if l.kind ~= "reg" then errors[#errors + 1] = "native value is not in register: " .. tostring(v); return nil end
    return l.reg
  end

  local function value_ty(v)
    return st.values and st.values[v] and st.values[v].ty or "Unknown"
  end

  local function input_slot(v)
    local l = loc[v]
    if not l then errors[#errors + 1] = "missing native slot value: " .. tostring(v); return nil end
    if l.kind ~= "slot" then errors[#errors + 1] = "native value is not a frame slot: " .. tostring(v); return nil end
    return l.hole
  end

  local function ensure_stub(n)
    local ep_idx = opts.exit_endpoint_by_node and opts.exit_endpoint_by_node[n.id]
    if not ep_idx then errors[#errors + 1] = "missing endpoint for native control op " .. tostring(n.id); return nil end
    return "exit_ep_" .. tostring(ep_idx), ep_idx
  end

  local stubs = {}
  local function record_stub(label, n)
    if not stubs[label] then stubs[label] = n end
  end

  local bool_label_counter = 0
  local shift_label_counter = 0

  local function reserve_temp_not(preferred, forbidden)
    forbidden = forbidden or {}
    for _, r in ipairs(REG_POOL) do
      if r ~= preferred and not forbidden[r] and free[r] then
        free[r] = nil
        return r
      end
    end
    return nil
  end

  local function emit_lua_shift(result_reg, count_reg, op)
    if not result_reg or not count_reg then return result_reg end
    if result_reg == "rcx" then
      local tmp = reserve_temp_not("rcx", { rcx = true, [count_reg] = count_reg ~= "rcx" })
      if not tmp then errors[#errors + 1] = "native x64 shift needs a non-rcx result register"; return result_reg end
      a:mov_r64_r64(tmp, result_reg)
      result_reg = tmp
    end
    if count_reg ~= "rcx" then a:mov_r64_r64("rcx", count_reg) end
    if op == "SHR" then a:neg_r64("rcx") end

    shift_label_counter = shift_label_counter + 1
    local neg = "shift_neg_" .. tostring(shift_label_counter)
    local zero = "shift_zero_" .. tostring(shift_label_counter)
    local done = "shift_done_" .. tostring(shift_label_counter)

    a:cmp_r64_imm8("rcx", 0)
    a:jcc32(0x8c, neg) -- JL: negative count means reverse direction.
    a:cmp_r64_imm8("rcx", 64)
    a:jcc32(0x83, zero) -- JAE: shifts >= NBITS produce zero.
    a:shift_r64_cl("shl", result_reg)
    a:jmp32(done)
    a:mark(neg)
    a:neg_r64("rcx")
    a:cmp_r64_imm8("rcx", 64)
    a:jcc32(0x83, zero)
    a:shift_r64_cl("shr", result_reg)
    a:jmp32(done)
    a:mark(zero)
    a:bin_r64_r64("xor", result_reg, result_reg)
    a:mark(done)
    return result_reg
  end

  local function store_tvalue(dst_hole, src_loc, src_value)
    if not dst_hole or not src_loc then return end
    local function dst_reloc(off, addend) make_data_reloc(data_relocs, off, "abs32s", dst_hole, addend) end

    if src_loc.kind == "slot" then
      local sh = src_loc.hole
      local off_src_v = a:mov_r64_m64("rax", "r11", slot_default_disp(sh, 0))
      make_data_reloc(data_relocs, off_src_v, "abs32s", sh, 0)
      local off_dst_v = a:mov_m64_r64("r11", slot_default_disp(dst_hole, 0), "rax")
      dst_reloc(off_dst_v, 0)
      local off_src_t = a:mov_r8_m8("rax", "r11", slot_default_disp(sh, 8))
      make_data_reloc(data_relocs, off_src_t, "abs32s", sh, 8)
      local off_dst_t = a:mov_m8_r8("r11", slot_default_disp(dst_hole, 8), "rax")
      dst_reloc(off_dst_t, 8)

    elseif src_loc.kind == "const_nil" then
      local off_v = a:mov_m64_imm32("r11", slot_default_disp(dst_hole, 0), 0)
      dst_reloc(off_v, 0)
      local off_t = a:mov_m8_imm8("r11", slot_default_disp(dst_hole, 8), 0)
      dst_reloc(off_t, 8)

    elseif src_loc.kind == "const_bool" then
      local off_v = a:mov_m64_imm32("r11", slot_default_disp(dst_hole, 0), 0)
      dst_reloc(off_v, 0)
      local default_tag = (src_loc.default == false) and 1 or 17
      local off_t, imm_off = a:mov_m8_imm8("r11", slot_default_disp(dst_hole, 8), default_tag)
      dst_reloc(off_t, 8)
      make_data_reloc(data_relocs, imm_off, "abs8", src_loc.hole, 0)

    elseif src_loc.kind == "const_pool" then
      local kh = src_loc.hole
      a:mov_r64_m64("r10", "rdi", CTX_K)
      local off_kv = a:mov_r64_m64("rax", "r10", slot_default_disp(kh, 0))
      make_data_reloc(data_relocs, off_kv, "abs32s", kh, 0)
      local off_dv = a:mov_m64_r64("r11", slot_default_disp(dst_hole, 0), "rax")
      dst_reloc(off_dv, 0)
      local off_kt = a:mov_r8_m8("rax", "r10", slot_default_disp(kh, 8))
      make_data_reloc(data_relocs, off_kt, "abs32s", kh, 8)
      local off_dt = a:mov_m8_r8("r11", slot_default_disp(dst_hole, 8), "rax")
      dst_reloc(off_dt, 8)

    elseif src_loc.kind == "reg" and value_ty(src_value) == "I64" then
      local off_v = a:mov_m64_r64("r11", slot_default_disp(dst_hole, 0), src_loc.reg)
      dst_reloc(off_v, 0)
      local off_t = a:mov_m8_imm8("r11", slot_default_disp(dst_hole, 8), LUA_VNUMINT)
      dst_reloc(off_t, 8)

    elseif src_loc.kind == "reg" and value_ty(src_value) == "F64" then
      local off_v = a:mov_m64_r64("r11", slot_default_disp(dst_hole, 0), src_loc.reg)
      dst_reloc(off_v, 0)
      local off_t = a:mov_m8_imm8("r11", slot_default_disp(dst_hole, 8), LUA_VNUMFLT)
      dst_reloc(off_t, 8)

    elseif src_loc.kind == "reg" and value_ty(src_value) == "Bool" then
      local off_v = a:mov_m64_imm32("r11", slot_default_disp(dst_hole, 0), 0)
      dst_reloc(off_v, 0)
      a:test_r64_r64(src_loc.reg)
      local off_false = a:mov_m8_imm8("r11", slot_default_disp(dst_hole, 8), 1)
      dst_reloc(off_false, 8)
      bool_label_counter = bool_label_counter + 1
      local done = "bool_store_done_" .. tostring(bool_label_counter)
      a:jcc32(0x84, done) -- JE: zero/false keeps false tag.
      local off_true = a:mov_m8_imm8("r11", slot_default_disp(dst_hole, 8), 17)
      dst_reloc(off_true, 8)
      a:mark(done)

    else
      errors[#errors + 1] = "unsupported native StoreSlot input: " .. tostring(src_loc.kind) .. ":" .. tostring(value_ty(src_value))
    end
  end

  -- Keep ctx in rdi for the full native stencil. r11 caches ctx->stack.
  a:mov_r64_m64("r11", "rdi", CTX_STACK)

  for _, n in ipairs(st.ops or {}) do
    local op = n.op
    if op == "LoadSlot" then
      for _, v in ipairs(n.outputs or {}) do loc[v] = { kind = "slot", hole = n.hole } end

    elseif op == "LoadConst" then
      for _, v in ipairs(n.outputs or {}) do loc[v] = { kind = "const_pool", hole = n.hole } end

    elseif op == "ConstNil" then
      for _, v in ipairs(n.outputs or {}) do loc[v] = { kind = "const_nil" } end

    elseif op == "ConstBool" then
      for _, v in ipairs(n.outputs or {}) do loc[v] = { kind = "const_bool", hole = n.hole, default = n.args and n.args.value } end

    elseif op == "Move" then
      local src = loc[n.inputs and n.inputs[1]]
      if not src then errors[#errors + 1] = "missing native move input: " .. tostring(n.inputs and n.inputs[1]) end
      for _, v in ipairs(n.outputs or {}) do loc[v] = src end
      for _, v in ipairs(n.inputs or {}) do consume(v) end

    elseif op == "NotTValue" then
      local h = input_slot(n.inputs and n.inputs[1])
      local r = alloc(n.outputs and n.outputs[1])
      if h and r then
        local off = a:mov_r8_m8(r, "r11", slot_default_disp(h, 8))
        make_data_reloc(data_relocs, off, "abs32s", h, 8)
        a:cmp_r8_imm8(r, 1)
        a:setcc_r8(0x96, r) -- SETBE: nil(0) or false(1) => true.
        a:movzx_r64_r8(r, r)
      end
      for _, v in ipairs(n.inputs or {}) do consume(v) end

    elseif op == "GuardI64" then
      local h = input_slot(n.inputs and n.inputs[1])
      if h then
        local off = a:cmp_m8_imm8("r11", slot_default_disp(h, 8), LUA_VNUMINT)
        make_data_reloc(data_relocs, off, "abs32s", h, 8)
        local label, ep_idx = ensure_stub(n)
        if label then
          local coff = a:jcc32(0x85, label) -- JNE
          control_relocs[#control_relocs + 1] = StencilDesc.control_reloc({
            code_offset = coff,
            code_offset_kind = "x86_64_rel32",
            reloc_kind = "pc32",
            edge_kind = "guard_fail",
            endpoint_index = ep_idx,
            source = n.source,
          })
          record_stub(label, n)
        end
      end
      for _, v in ipairs(n.inputs or {}) do consume(v) end

    elseif op == "UnboxI64" then
      local h = input_slot(n.inputs and n.inputs[1])
      local r = alloc(n.outputs and n.outputs[1])
      if h and r then
        local off = a:mov_r64_m64(r, "r11", slot_default_disp(h, 0))
        make_data_reloc(data_relocs, off, "abs32s", h, 0)
      end
      for _, v in ipairs(n.inputs or {}) do consume(v) end

    elseif op == "ConstI64" then
      local r = alloc(n.outputs and n.outputs[1])
      if r then a:movabs_r64(r, tonumber(n.args and n.args.value) or 0) end

    elseif op == "ConstI64Hole" then
      local r = alloc(n.outputs and n.outputs[1])
      if r then
        local off = a:movabs_r64(r, 0)
        make_data_reloc(data_relocs, off, "abs64", n.hole, 0)
      end

    elseif op == "ConstF64" then
      local r = alloc(n.outputs and n.outputs[1])
      if r then a:movabs_r64_bytes(r, f64_bytes_from_int(tonumber(n.args and n.args.value) or 0)) end

    elseif op == "ConstF64Hole" then
      local r = alloc(n.outputs and n.outputs[1])
      if r then
        local off = a:movabs_r64(r, 0)
        make_data_reloc(data_relocs, off, "abs64", n.hole, 0)
      end

    elseif op == "AddI64" or op == "SubI64" or op == "MulI64" or op == "I64BinOp" then
      local lhs = input_reg(n.inputs and n.inputs[1])
      local rhs = input_reg(n.inputs and n.inputs[2])
      local bop = (op == "AddI64" and "ADD") or (op == "SubI64" and "SUB") or (op == "MulI64" and "MUL") or (n.args and n.args.op)
      local xop = BINOP[bop]
      if (bop == "SHL" or bop == "SHR") and lhs and rhs then
        local out = n.outputs and n.outputs[1]
        local out_reg = emit_lua_shift(lhs, rhs, bop)
        loc[out] = { kind = "reg", reg = out_reg }
      elseif not xop then errors[#errors + 1] = "unsupported native x64 i64 binop: " .. tostring(bop)
      elseif lhs and rhs then
        local out = n.outputs and n.outputs[1]
        loc[out] = { kind = "reg", reg = lhs }
        a:bin_r64_r64(xop, lhs, rhs)
      end
      consume(n.inputs and n.inputs[1]); consume(n.inputs and n.inputs[2])

    elseif op == "I64UnaryOp" then
      local src = input_reg(n.inputs and n.inputs[1])
      local uop = n.args and n.args.op
      if src then
        loc[n.outputs and n.outputs[1]] = { kind = "reg", reg = src }
        a:unary_r64(uop, src)
      end
      consume(n.inputs and n.inputs[1])

    elseif op == "CmpI64" then
      local lhs = input_reg(n.inputs and n.inputs[1])
      local rhs = input_reg(n.inputs and n.inputs[2])
      local cc = CMP_CC[n.args and n.args.cmp_op or "EQ"]
      if not cc then errors[#errors + 1] = "unsupported native x64 i64 cmp: " .. tostring(n.args and n.args.cmp_op)
      elseif lhs and rhs then
        loc[n.outputs and n.outputs[1]] = { kind = "reg", reg = lhs }
        a:cmp_r64_r64(lhs, rhs)
        a:setcc_r8(cc, lhs)
        a:movzx_r64_r8(lhs, lhs)
      end
      consume(n.inputs and n.inputs[1]); consume(n.inputs and n.inputs[2])

    elseif op == "StoreSlot" then
      local input = n.inputs and n.inputs[1]
      store_tvalue(n.hole, loc[input], input)
      for _, v in ipairs(n.inputs or {}) do consume(v) end

    elseif op == "StoreI64Slot" then
      local src = input_reg(n.inputs and n.inputs[1])
      local h = n.hole
      if src and h then
        local off_v = a:mov_m64_r64("r11", slot_default_disp(h, 0), src)
        make_data_reloc(data_relocs, off_v, "abs32s", h, 0)
        local off_t = a:mov_m8_imm8("r11", slot_default_disp(h, 8), LUA_VNUMINT)
        make_data_reloc(data_relocs, off_t, "abs32s", h, 8)
      end
      for _, v in ipairs(n.inputs or {}) do consume(v) end

    elseif op == "ExitBoundary" then
      local label, ep_idx = ensure_stub(n)
      if label then
        local coff = a:jmp32(label)
        control_relocs[#control_relocs + 1] = StencilDesc.control_reloc({
          code_offset = coff,
          code_offset_kind = "x86_64_rel32",
          reloc_kind = "pc32",
          edge_kind = control_edge_kind(n),
          endpoint_index = ep_idx,
          source = n.source,
        })
        record_stub(label, n)
      end
      for _, v in ipairs(n.inputs or {}) do consume(v) end

    else
      errors[#errors + 1] = "unsupported native x64 stencil op: " .. tostring(op)
    end
  end

  -- Success is an explicit patchable fallthrough edge. If a materializer does
  -- not patch it, the rel32 targets the local ok endpoint stub below.
  local ok_endpoint_index = opts.ok_endpoint_index or 2
  local ok_label = "ok_ep_" .. tostring(ok_endpoint_index)
  local ok_off = a:jmp32(ok_label)
  control_relocs[#control_relocs + 1] = StencilDesc.control_reloc({
    code_offset = ok_off,
    code_offset_kind = "x86_64_rel32",
    reloc_kind = "pc32",
    edge_kind = "fallthrough",
    endpoint_index = ok_endpoint_index,
    source = 0,
  })

  a:mark(ok_label)
  a:mov_m32_imm32("rdi", CTX_EXIT_KIND, SPON_EXIT_NONE)
  a:ret()

  local labels = {}
  for label in pairs(stubs) do labels[#labels + 1] = label end
  table.sort(labels)
  for _, label in ipairs(labels) do
    local n = stubs[label]
    local source = tonumber(n.source or 0) or 0
    a:mark(label)
    a:mov_m32_imm32("rdi", CTX_EXIT_KIND, op_exit_kind(n))
    a:mov_m32_imm32("rdi", CTX_EXIT_PC, source)
    a:mov_m32_imm32("rdi", CTX_EXIT_OP_IDX, math.max(0, source - 1))
    a:mov_m32_imm32("rdi", CTX_EXIT_HOLE, 0)

[35 more lines in file. Use offset=701 to continue.]

a:ret()
  end

  for _, e in ipairs(errors) do a:error(e) end
  local bytes, ferrs = a:finish()
  if not bytes then return nil, ferrs end

  return {
    bytes = bytes,
    code_hex = bytes_to_hex(bytes),
    code_size = #bytes,
    data_relocs = data_relocs,
    control_relocs = control_relocs,
    layout = {
      mode = "native_bytes",
      executable = true,
      arch = "x86_64",
      encoding = "raw_function_body",
      entry_offset = 0,
      code_size = #bytes,
      fixed_width_patch_sites = true,
    },
  }
end

M.CTX_OFFSETS = {
  stack = CTX_STACK,
  exit_kind = CTX_EXIT_KIND,
  exit_pc = CTX_EXIT_PC,
  exit_op_idx = CTX_EXIT_OP_IDX,
  exit_hole = CTX_EXIT_HOLE,
}

return M

content.thinking:
**Clarifying test generation**

I need to generate tests for IDIV/MOD that are easier to handle. I'm thinking about how they should behave, especially regarding divisors. If the divisor is zero, it has to be prevented from computing, as that leads to a runtime error. I need to consider whether I should enforce a requirement for a nonzero integer. Currently, the global signature for nonzero integers isn’t specific to the slots. I might need to inspect if the facts support nonzero values in different contexts.
